commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the auth-provider compatibility tradeoff, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff narrows an existing unbounded auth response read and does not add dependencies, permissions, scripts, secret exposure, or new code execution surface. (sha=464a901534afada03e5467daa27d523b78dbc33e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps GitHub Copilot token exchange success JSON parsing in the shared bounded provider reader and adds a regression test for oversized streamed success bodies.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +45. Total +48 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.10 still call unbounded res.json() on the Copilot token success response, and Node's Undici implementation fully reads body bytes before JSON.parse. I did not run a live reproduction because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the auth-provider compatibility tradeoff, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): bound Copilot token exchange JSON response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where the GitHub Copilot API token exchange success response
was read with an unbounded `await res.json()`, meaning a misbehaving,
compromised, or proxied Copilot token endpoint could stream an arbitrarily
large JSON body into the Node.js heap before parsing began — causing memory
pressure or OOM during `openclaw onboard` and every Copilot auth refresh.

The failure path at `provider-auth.ts:309` already calls
`cancelUnreadResponseBody(res)` to release the stream, but the success path
immediately below had no size cap — a clear asymmetric gap.

This is a **SDK-level** path (`src/plugin-sdk/`): every consumer of
`resolveCopilotApiToken` (agent startup, onboarding, live Copilot inference
sessions) is affected. Sibling paths in the same campaign that have already
been fixed include `github-copilot/usage.ts` and `github-copilot/embeddings.ts`
(merged #96607), and `github-copilot/login.ts` device-flow exchange.

## Why This Change Was Made

`readProviderJsonResponse` (from `../agents/provider-http-errors.js`, the same
module re-exported via `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`) wraps
`readResponseWithLimit` with a 16 MiB cap, cancels the ReadableStream on
overflow, and wraps JSON parse errors in a consistent
`"<label>: malformed JSON response"` message.

The call becomes:

```typescript
// Before
const json = parseCopilotTokenResponse(await res.json());

// After
const json = parseCopilotTokenResponse(
  await readProviderJsonResponse(res, "github-copilot.token"),
);
```

The `parseCopilotTokenResponse` wrapper is unchanged — it still validates the
`token` and `expires_at` fields after the bounded read. Net diff: **+1 import,
+2 production lines** (wrapping the existing single-line call).

## User Impact

No user-visible behavior change for well-behaved GitHub Copilot endpoints.
Operators or users whose Copilot token endpoint returns an oversized JSON
payload (e.g., a misconfigured proxy, a MITM relay, or a future protocol
change) will now see a bounded error
(`github-copilot.token: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes`) instead of
unbounded heap growth.

## Evidence

**Diff** (`git show HEAD --stat`):

```
 src/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.test.ts | 45 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 src/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.ts      |  5 +++-
 2 files changed, 49 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
```

**Proof script** (`node scripts/proof-copilot-token-bound.mjs`, Node v22.22.0):

```
[case 1] oversized Copilot token JSON, cap=1 MiB, 64×1 MiB chunks
  ok: readResponseWithLimit rejected on oversized body — true
  ok: error message contains size info (got: Content too large: 2097152 bytes (limit: 1048576 bytes)) — true
  ok: stream cancelled before all 64 chunks read (readCount=2) — true
  ok: stream cancel() was invoked — true

[negative control] OLD unbounded await response.json() — no cancellation
  ok: unbounded .json() consumed all 64 chunks before failing (readCount=64) — true
  ok: stream NOT cancelled via .cancel() — drained to EOF instead — true

[case 3] small valid Copilot token JSON — parses unchanged
  ok: small body parses successfully — true
  ok: token field intact — true
  ok: expires_at field intact — true

ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED
```

**New test added** — `"bounds oversized Copilot token success body and cancels the stream"`:
- Builds a 64 × 1 MiB `ReadableStream` backed `Response`
- Asserts rejection + `readCount < 64` + `canceled === true`
- Complements the existing `"cancels Copilot token exchange error bodies"` test
  at line 340 which already validates the failure-path cancel behavior

**Local test suite** (`pnpm test src/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.test.ts --reporter=verbose`,
Vitest v4.1.8, Node v22.22.0):

```
 ✓  accepts plus-signed Copilot token expiry strings
 ✓  rejects malformed Copilot proxy hints
 ✓  rejects Copilot token expiry values outside the supported date range
 ✓  cancels Copilot token exchange error bodies
 ✓  bounds oversized Copilot token success body and cancels the stream   ← new
 ✓  refreshes cached Copilot tokens with out-of-range expiry values
 ... (9 passed total)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  9 passed (9)
  Duration  5.72s
```

AI-assisted.
target.author: hugenshen
